welcome.title: Hey, welcome!
welcome.description: Start fresh with your own data, or explore the app with sample data first.

tour.skipDescription: You haven't opened the {module} tab yet. Skip to the next module?

steps.tx1: All your financial records are here: income, expenses, and transfers between wallets. Tap this tab to start.
steps.tx2: Swipe left/right or tap the arrows to navigate between dates. Tap the date to open the calendar.
steps.tx3: Today's total income, expenses, and balance are shown here.
steps.tx4: Tap to add a new transaction. Pick Income, Expense, or Transfer.
steps.tx5: Tap to change the sort order, by date or amount.
steps.tx6: Tap to export all transactions to an Excel file.
steps.tx7: Tap to view the full transaction history across all dates.
steps.wl1: Wallets are your accounts and cash. All transactions are linked to a wallet. Tap this tab to continue.
steps.wl2: This card shows the total balance across all your wallets.
steps.wl3: Filter wallets by type: bank, e-wallet, savings, and more.
steps.wl4: Sort wallets by name or balance to find them quickly.
steps.wl5: Tap to add a new wallet: bank account, cash, GoPay, and more.
steps.wl6: Tap a wallet card to view details, edit balance, or see transaction history.
steps.ln1: Track who owes you and who you owe, all in one place. Tap this tab to continue.
steps.ln2: 'Give': you lend money to someone, or you pay off your own debt. 'Receive': you get money from someone, or they pay you back.
steps.ln3: Everyone is grouped here. Tap a name to see their loan history.
steps.rp1: Your financial summary is here: income, expenses, and balance. Tap this tab to continue.
steps.rp2: View reports by period: live today, monthly, or a custom date range.
steps.rp3: Switch between Expense and Income to see the breakdown chart for each.
steps.rp4: This chart shows expenses or income by category. Tap a slice to filter the list below.
steps.rp5: Tap to create a custom report for a specific date range.
steps.end: That's it! You know your way around PFinTrack now. Start recording.

changelog.title: What's New
changelog.subtitle: The story behind every PFinTrack update

entries.intro: Small touches that make every interaction feel more natural.
items: Wallet balances always stay in sync when money moves between them
Accessibility labels now follow your language choice
Icon badges and quick menus look consistent across the app
App loads faster and stays stable from the first second

entries.intro: A round of small fixes before things get interesting.
items: The onboarding tour looks cleaner and more focused
Confirmation dialogs feel more natural and work better with screen readers
Switching tabs in Reports is instant
Long lists in Wallet & Loan scroll more smoothly

entries.intro: PFinTrack is live. Manage your personal finances from one place.
items: Track wallets, edit balances manually, and follow every change
Log income, expenses, and transfers between wallets
Manage loans with automatic settlement detection
Real-time, monthly, and custom financial reports
Backup, theme, language, and demo data, all in one place
Works offline and installs like a native app

items.a: All your financial data (wallets, transactions, loans) lives on your device, inside your browser's local storage. PFinTrack has no server and never sends your data anywhere. It's yours, on your device.

items.q: What happens if I switch phones or lose my device?
items.a: Since your data lives on your device, switching phones without a backup means losing it. Before you switch: Settings → Data & Storage → Export. Save the file somewhere safe, like Google Drive or your gallery. Then import it on your new device.

items.q: Can PFinTrack see my financial data?
items.a: No. PFinTrack has no accounts, no login, and no server. Your data is 100% on your device. Nobody can access it, including the PFinTrack team. We don't even know you exist.

items.q: How do I back up and restore my data?
items.a: Settings → Data & Storage. Export saves your data as a .json or .gz file. Import brings it back. Back up regularly, especially before switching phones or doing a major browser update.

items.q: How do I delete all my data?
items.a: Settings → Data & Storage → delete all data. This permanently removes all wallets, transactions, and loan records. Export a backup first if you might need the data later.

items.q: What's the difference between Give and Receive in the Loan module?
items.a: Give means you lent money to someone, so they owe you. Receive means you got money from them, either they're paying you back, or you're paying off a debt. Everything is grouped by person so you can track it all in one view.

items.q: Why doesn't a Transfer count as an expense?
items.a: A transfer just moves money between your own wallets. Your net worth stays the same. It's different from an expense, where money actually leaves. Counting it as an expense would make your reports look worse than reality.

items.q: What is "Balance Correction" in my transaction list?
items.a: It's an automatic entry created when you manually edit a wallet's balance. For example, if you change your BCA balance from Rp 500,000 to Rp 750,000, PFinTrack records the Rp 250,000 difference as a Balance Correction so the change is always traceable.

items.q: What is Saving Rate and how is it calculated?
items.a: Saving Rate shows what percentage of your income you actually kept. The formula: (Income − Expenses) ÷ Income × 100%. If income is Rp 10 million and expenses Rp 7 million, your Saving Rate is 30%. Higher is better.

items.q: Why can my wallet balance go negative?
items.a: It happens when you record an expense or loan that exceeds a wallet's balance. PFinTrack doesn't block it because that's real life. Credit cards and overdraft accounts can genuinely go negative. A negative balance isn't an error, it's valid data.

items.q: Can I use PFinTrack without an internet connection?
items.a: Yes, fully. PFinTrack is a PWA (Progressive Web App). Install it and use it 100% offline. All features work without internet: logging transactions, viewing reports, managing wallets. You only need a connection the very first time you open the app.

Demo Mode fills the app with fake data (wallets, transactions, and loans) so you can explore all features without entering real data. Turn it on from Settings, and clear it anytime using the red button in Settings when you're ready to start fresh.

items.q: How do I restart the guided tour?
items.a: Settings → scroll to the Help section → tap "View Tutorial". The tour restarts from the beginning and walks you through all the main features again.
Yes. PFinTrack is completely free. No premium tier, no subscription, no ads, no in-app purchases. Every feature, now and in future updates, is free for everyone.
